casualties: Roman Republic: unknown; Nervii-led Belgae: about 59,500 casualties

title: Battle of Morbihan
casualties: Roman Republic: light losses; Veneti: almost all ships destroyed or captured

title: Battle of Gergovia

title: Battle of Heiligerlee
casualties: Dutch rebels: 50 dead or wounded; Spanish Empire: 460 dead
status: First Dutch rebel victory of the Eighty Years War.

title: Battle of Jemmingen
casualties: Dutch rebels: 7,000 dead or wounded; Spanish Empire: 8 dead and 220 wounded

title: Siege of Haarlem
casualties: Dutch rebels and relief forces: about 2,700-7,000 dead or wounded; Spain: at least 1,700 dead plus thousands of casualties
status: Costly Spanish siege that became an Orangist symbol.

title: Siege of Leiden
casualties: Dutch rebels: about 500 casualties; Spain: about 2,000 casualties
status: Dutch relief of Leiden after flooding the polders.

title: Battle of Mookerheyde
casualties: Dutch rebels: about 3,000 dead or wounded; Spain: about 150 dead or wounded
status: Spanish victory that killed two Nassau brothers.

title: Fall of Antwerp
casualties: States-General: about 8,000 casualties; Spain: about 1,800 casualties
status: Major Spanish siege and capture of Antwerp.

title: Battle of Turnhout
casualties: Spanish Empire: about 2,000 killed or wounded and 500-700 captured; Dutch Republic and England: 12 killed and 50 wounded
status: Anglo-Dutch cavalry victory over Spanish infantry.

title: Siege of 's-Hertogenbosch
casualties: Spain: 1,200 killed and 1,200 diseased or injured; Dutch Republic: unknown
status: Dutch capture of a major Spanish stronghold.

title: Fall of Granada
status: Final capture of Granada and endpoint of the Reconquista.

title: Siege of the Alcazar
status: Early symbolic Nationalist victory in Toledo.

status: Republican defense and long Nationalist siege of the capital.

status: Failed Nationalist attempt to cut Madrid's Valencia road.

status: Republican defeat of Italian and Nationalist forces near Madrid.

title: Battle of Brunete
status: Republican offensive west of Madrid that ended in costly reversal.

title: Battle of the Ebro
status: Largest and last major Republican offensive.

title: Siege of Detroit
status: British and Indigenous capture of Detroit early in the War of 1812.

title: Burning of Washington
status: British raid and burning of federal buildings in Washington, D.C.

title: Battle of Baltimore
status: Defense of Baltimore and Fort McHenry, source of the national anthem.

title: Battle of Copenhagen (1801)
casualties: Royal Navy: 274 killed and 737 wounded; Denmark-Norway: estimates vary, including 367 killed, 635 wounded, and 1,179 captured
status: Nelson's costly attack on the Dano-Norwegian fleet during the French Revolutionary Wars.

title: Battle of Copenhagen (1807)
casualties: Britain: 42 killed and 145 wounded; Denmark-Norway: military casualties plus about 195 civilians killed and 768 civilians injured
status: British bombardment and seizure of the Dano-Norwegian fleet during the Napoleonic Wars.

title: Mystic massacre
casualties: Pequot: about 400-700 killed; Connecticut, Narragansett, and Mohegan forces: light losses
status: Pequot War attack also known as the Battle of Mystic Fort.

title: Battle of Bloody Brook
casualties: Pocumtuc-led Native forces: unknown; New England Confederation and Mohegan allies: at least 58 militia and 16 civilians killed
status: King Philip's War ambush of a colonial wagon train.

title: Great Swamp Fight
casualties: New England Confederation and Native allies: about 70 killed and 150 wounded; Narragansett: about 97 warriors and 300-1,000 noncombatants killed, 300 captured
status: Major King Philip's War assault on a Narragansett winter fort.

title: Battle of Bushy Run
casualties: Delaware, Shawnee, Mingo, and Huron forces: about 20-60 killed; Great Britain: about 50 killed and 60 wounded
status: British victory during Pontiac's War that relieved Fort Pitt.

title: Battle of Fallen Timbers
casualties: United States: 33 killed and 100 wounded; Northwestern Confederacy: about 25-40 killed
status: Decisive Northwest Indian War battle before the Treaty of Greenville.

title: Battle of Tippecanoe
casualties: Tecumseh's Confederacy: about 36-65 killed, 70-80 wounded, and 1 captured; United States: 62 killed and 126 wounded
status: Battle between Harrison's force and Tenskwatawa's followers during Tecumseh's War.

title: Sand Creek massacre
casualties: United States: 24 killed and 52 wounded; Cheyenne and Arapaho: estimates range from about 70 to 600 killed
status: U.S. Army attack on a Cheyenne and Arapaho village during the Colorado War.

title: Battle of the Washita River
casualties: United States: 21 killed and 13 wounded; Cheyenne: estimates range from 13 to 150 killed, with 53 women and children captured
status: Custer's attack on Black Kettle's Southern Cheyenne camp.

title: Battle of the Rosebud
casualties: Lakota, Cheyenne, and Arapaho: about 13-36 killed and 63-100 wounded; United States, Crow, and Shoshoni: about 15-36 killed and 43-46 wounded
status: Great Sioux War battle that delayed Crook before Little Bighorn.

title: Battle of Bear Paw
casualties: United States: 24 dead and 49 wounded; Nez Perce: 25 killed, 46 wounded, and 431 surrendered or captured
status: Final engagement of the Nez Perce War.

title: Wounded Knee Massacre
casualties: United States: 25-31 killed and 33-39 wounded; Lakota: about 250-300 killed and 51 wounded
status: 1890 massacre of Lakota people by U.S. Army troops at Wounded Knee Creek.

title: Battle of Yatay
casualties: Triple Alliance: 147 killed and 560 wounded; Paraguay: 1,700 killed, 300 wounded, and 1,200 captured
status: First major land battle of the Paraguayan War.

title: Battle of Curupayty
casualties: Paraguay: 54 killed and 38 wounded; Triple Alliance: 2,225 Argentine casualties and 2,002 Brazilian casualties
status: Major Paraguayan defensive victory against the Triple Alliance.

title: Siege of Humaitá
status: Key fortress siege that opened the route into Paraguay.

title: Battle of Lomas Valentinas
casualties: Paraguay: 1,500 killed and 1,500 captured; Triple Alliance: 3,270 casualties
status: Decisive Allied victory in the Pikysyry campaign.

title: Battle of Acosta Ñu
casualties: Paraguay: 2,000 killed and 1,500 wounded or captured; Brazil and Argentina: 182 killed and 420 wounded
status: Late-war battle remembered in Paraguay as the Children's Battle.
